DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS JAMES YUEH AND U.S.CONTINENTAL INVESTMENT REALTY CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with Leave to Amend. Defendants did not have a duty to disclose to Plaintiffs that a "tenant was not legally required to vacate" by the close of escrow for 32-24 Clement Street. (Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint, Attachment FR-3.) Plaintiff's claim for fraud does not meet heightened pleading requirements, even as against a fiduciary. Defendants first represented that Mario Previtali would move out by the close of escrow. Defendants then filed an Extension of Time Addendum to the Commercial property Purchase Agreement that stated Mario Previtali would move out of 32-34 Clement Street one month after escrow closed. Defendants' First Amended Complaint does not sufficiently set forth facts to determine whether this change in circumstances is actionable. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
